steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a transitions question. The first sentence calls Old Faithful "seemingly inhospitable to life;" the second reveals it is "almost like a cradle for biodiversity." The blank must signal that the second fact is true despite the first — a concessive (contrast) transition.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Thus,’: Signals a result or consequence; implies the biodiversity follows logically from the inhospitable conditions. That is the opposite of what the passage means. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Specifically,’: Signals that what follows narrows or elaborates on the prior claim; implies the biodiversity is a specific example of inhospitability rather than a contradiction of it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Still,’: Means "nevertheless" — signals that the geyser's thriving biodiversity is true despite its seemingly inhospitable appearance. Correctly marks the concession. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘In other words,’: Signals a restatement or paraphrase; implies the biodiversity restates the inhospitability claim rather than contradicting it. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
